info.title: HEVN API
info.description: Backend API for HEVN mobile neobank

post.description: Start authentication: verify TEE signature, then send OTP to email.
Verify email OTP code. Sets email_otp_validated flag on session.
Verify TOTP code. Sets totp_validated flag on session.

Verify TEE-signed Google JWT attestation. Sets tee_validated flag on session.

Checks:
- msg in auth_payload == session_key (binds attestation to this session)
- hash_email matches session email
- Ed25519 signature is valid using TEE_PUBLIC_KEY
Complete auth: issue refresh token if all required steps are validated.

Authenticate via Privy embedded wallet signature.

1. Recover signer from signature of "I am the owner of {email}"
2. Verify via Privy API that this address is the embedded wallet for the email
3. Get or create user, save privy_address, deploy smart wallet if needed
4. Return HEVN refresh token

Exchange a refresh token for a short-lived session token (10 min).

If user_id differs from the token owner, checks team membership
and issues a delegated session token (sub=owner, uid=target).

put.description: Create or update user profile data for KYC.

get.description: Get the profile of the currently authenticated user.

post.description: Get Swipelux KYC verification link.

get.description: Check the current KYC verification status. Use provider=align for Align.

schema.title: Response Geocode Address Api V1 User Geo Get

post.description: Proxy user events to Customer.io for analytics tracking.

schema.title: Response Track User Event Api V1 User Event Post

get.description: Get all devices associated with the authenticated user's account.

schema.title: Response List Devices Api V1 Devices Get

put.description: Set or update the push notification token for the current device. Pass an empty pushToken to unsubscribe the device. Web subscriptions arrive as JSON-stringified PushSubscription; native tokens (APNs/FCM) are short strings.

get.description: Return recent notifications attributed to the current user. Each item carries its type, the originating device label, and the client IP captured at trigger time (when available).

post.description: Read-only KYC info for the rail. Does not mutate Bank rows.
Create or upsert a Bank row for the given rail. Idempotent.

post.summary: Get a pooled bank payin quote
post.description: Create a Swipelux quote for paying into the user's custodial wallet via a pooled bank rail.

post.summary: Submit a pooled bank payin quote
post.description: Execute a Swipelux payin quote and return payment instructions.

post.summary: Cancel a locally tracked bank payin quote
post.description: Mark the local FiatTransfer created from a Swipelux payin quote as canceled.
Create or upsert Bank rows for multiple rails in one request. Returns a per-rail result so one pending or failed rail does not abort the batch.

get.description: Return every supported bank rail. Rails the user has activated come from the Bank table (with status / iban_data); rails the user has not activated are returned from the static BANK_INFO registry with status=not_started and iban_data=None. If a rail's provider KYC is APPROVED but no ACTIVE Bank row exists yet, syncs lazily from Swipelux / Align before returning.
Generate and return account confirmation PDF for a Bank row.
Generate and return account statement PDF with transactions for the period.

get.summary: Get transaction receipt PDF download link
get.description: Generate receipt PDF for a transaction, upload to Supabase, return download link.

schema.title: Response Get Transaction Receipt Pdf Api V1 Export Transaction Receipt  Transaction Id  Get

get.description: Verify a generated PDF document by its internal ID and return metadata.

post.description: Extract SEPA IBAN data from plain text using AI. Returns structured ImportIbanSepaData.

get.description: Public endpoint. Returns FX rates from the given currency to USD: `bankTransfer` (Swipelux), `card` (Wirex), and `usdRate` (exchangerate-api.com). `usdRate` is populated for every currency in the invoice Currency enum (G10 + AED/HKD).
Look up bank/branch info by BIC code via API Ninjas.
Look up US ABA/RTN routing number via API Ninjas.
Validate IBAN and return bank/account info via API Ninjas.

post.description: Receive KYC webhook payload; log body and return 200.
Handle Swipelux webhooks: customer.verification_changed, transfer.*.
Handle Hook0 Base SpendPermission events.
Process Coinbase CDP transfer webhooks.
Process Align webhook (customer.kycs.updated, virtual_account.* etc.).
Handle Resend email webhooks with Svix signature verification.
Handle Firma e-signature webhooks.

Handle Privy webhook events (Svix-signed).

Security-relevant events trigger email alerts to the affected user.

post.description: Create a quote to deposit from any chain to Base USDC.

Materialize a 1click PAYIN row by deposit address. The quote is re-fetched from /v0/status (no quote state is kept on the backend). Status, origin_tx_hash and destination_tx_hash get filled in by the background 1click explorer sync once the deposit lands.
Get quote for payout to contact. Auto-detects payment channel (IBAN/onchain/internal).
Estimate payout amount and fees without a contact or provider-side activation.
Activate a payout quote and create pending records. Returns deposit address.

post.summary: Deploy Safe 2/2 multisig with passkey + backend EOA
post.description: Deploys a Safe v1.4.1 wallet with threshold 2/2. Owners are the backend EOA (from config) and a SafeWebAuthnSigner contract derived from the provided passkey (x, y) P-256 coordinates.

`eth_sendUserOperation` is handled by `paymaster_raw`.
All other methods are proxied to the Coinbase paymaster URL.

get.description: Volume + count grouped by tag or type, split into time buckets across the fromDate..toDate range. Granularity is auto-picked: >6 months → month, >30 days → week, otherwise day. Empty periods are returned as zero-filled buckets so the frontend gets a complete x-axis. Same filter set as /transactions and /transactions/breakdown. `fromDate` and `toDate` are required.

get.description: Authenticated owners get the full UX (status auto-sync, document download links, crypto→internal repair). Unauthenticated callers must pass `?key=` with a share key issued by `POST /transactions/{id}/share`. Without auth and without a valid key the endpoint returns 401.

post.summary: Create a share link for a transaction
post.description: Owner-only. Creates a temp access key scoped to this transaction_id. Pass the raw key back to `GET /transactions/{id}/details?key=<key>` to fetch the receipt without auth.
Set or clear a tag on a transaction.

put.description: Set or clear a memo (description) on a transaction.

post.summary: Attach a document to a transaction
post.description: Append an already-uploaded document (by id) to the transaction's attachments. The document must belong to the same user. Idempotent — re-attaching an existing id is a no-op. Returns the full updated list with signed download links.

schema.title: Response Attach Document To Transaction Api V1 Transactions  Transaction Id  Attachments Post

post.description:
Google JWT attestation via Nitro Enclave.

Client encrypts {google_jwt, msg, receiver_id} with enclave's Curve25519 key,
sends the ciphertext here. Enclave decrypts, verifies JWT, and returns a signed attestation.

get.description: Get the enclave's Curve25519 encryption public key for E2E encrypted requests.
Get Google JWKS certs signed by enclave — allows clients to verify enclave integrity.
Get the signing public key (ed25519:base58).

post.description:
Verify Google JWT and return signed attestation.

Same logic as enclave GOOGLE_ATTESTATION handler, but accepts plaintext
(no NaCl Box encryption) and signs with near_wallet key.

Execute w_execute_signed transaction using near_wallet key.

Accepts {msg, publicKey, proof} from the frontend, extracts receiver_id
(wallet address) from msg.signer_id.

get.description: Start Xero OAuth flow. Returns redirect to Xero login.

OAuth callback from Xero. Exchanges code for tokens.

Note: This endpoint is called by Xero redirect, so we look up the user
from the stored OAuth state rather than requiring a JWT header.

post.summary: Send USDC from app.smartWalletAddress, signed by the api-key EOA. Requires X-Api-Key with scope including 'apps-transfer'.
post.description:
Resolve recipient + amount from context, then spend USDC from the
app's smart wallet via the user's SpendPermission.

Mirrors the body of ``POST /mcp/transfer``; the only differences are
the auth model (new ``api_keys`` instead of ``mcp_api_keys``) and the
spender wallet (shared ``app.smart_wallet_address`` instead of per-key).

AppTransferContext.title: AppTransferContext
AppTransferContext.description:
Body of POST /apps/{app_id}/transfer.

Exactly one of ``contactId`` / ``quoteId`` / ``invoiceId`` MUST be set
(mirrors the /mcp/transfer body). ``amount`` is required when
``contactId`` is the dispatch path; otherwise it's derived from the
referenced invoice / quote.

A call without ``finalize`` creates the period invoice as a DRAFT (or
updates its items if a DRAFT already exists). Both the client and the
contractor may edit a DRAFT. Setting ``finalize=True`` additionally moves
the invoice to SENT, generates the PDF and emails the client — only the
client (contract owner) is allowed to finalize.

CreateInvoiceFromContractRequest.title: CreateInvoiceFromContractRequest
CreateInvoiceFromContractRequest.description:
Request to create an invoice from a contract.

Either party (client or contractor) of the contract may create the invoice;
attribution always flows from ``contract.contractor`` to
``contract.client``. Amount defaults to the contract amount when not
provided. ``contractor_address`` is optional — when omitted, the snapshot
stored on the contract (or the linked contact's address) is used.
``invoice_number`` is auto-generated per (contractor, client) pair as e.g.
``"INV-0001"``.

discount.title: Discount
discount.description: Fixed discount in invoice currency. Subtracted from sum of items.

CreateInvoiceRequest.title: CreateInvoiceRequest
CreateInvoiceRequest.description:
Request to create an invoice.

Participant data (email, display_name, address) is snapshotted at creation time.
Amount is computed as sum of items[].price * items[].quantity.
invoice_number is auto-generated per (contractor, client) pair.

CreateUploadedInvoiceRequest.title: CreateUploadedInvoiceRequest
CreateUploadedInvoiceRequest.description:

Current user is the client/payer. Payment methods are resolved from saved
contacts; direct paymentMethods input is intentionally not accepted.

IBANStatus.title: IBANStatus
IBANStatus.description:
IBAN account status.

State machine:
``not_started`` → ``requested`` → ``document_requested`` → ``approved`` → ``pending`` → ``active``

- ``not_started``       — default for every rail until the user opts in.
- ``requested``         — user asked for this rail via /pre-approve; awaiting KYC.
- ``document_requested``— admin asked the user for more documents (KYB).
- ``approved``          — KYB passed, account ready to be created at provider.
- ``pending_kyc``       — provider rejected creation until KYC is approved.
- ``pending``           — provider accepted/created the account, waiting.
- ``active``            — real bank details are attached to the row.
- ``frozen`` / ``closed``— terminal/blocked states.

IbanInfoResponse.description: IBAN validation response from Fincodes API.

PaymasterRawRequest.title: PaymasterRawRequest
PaymasterRawRequest.description:
A fully-built ERC-4337 v0.6 `UserOperation` to push through
`EntryPoint.handleOps` directly from the backend EOA.

The backend acts as the bundler itself: it ABI-encodes
`handleOps([userOp], beneficiary)` against the canonical v0.6
EntryPoint and broadcasts an EIP-1559 tx from the CDP "owner"
server account, paying gas in ETH. There is no third-party
paymaster / bundler service involvement.

`userOp` follows the JSON-RPC `eth_sendUserOperation` shape exactly
— camelCase keys, all numeric fields as `0x`-hex strings, all byte
fields as `0x`-hex strings, signature already applied. See
`UserOperationV06` for the full field list.

PaymasterRawResponse.title: PaymasterRawResponse
PaymasterRawResponse.description:
Response for /wallet/paymaster_raw.

The backend EOA submitted `EntryPoint.handleOps([userOp], beneficiary)`
on Base mainnet and waited for the receipt. All fields reflect the
on-chain outcome — including reverts. A `status == 0` means the tx
was mined but the EntryPoint reverted (e.g. `AA24 signature error`,
`AA33 reverted (or OOG)`); the tx hash is still real and can be
inspected on basescan.

TemplateDescriptor.title: TemplateDescriptor
TemplateDescriptor.description: Describes a document template and the fields a client must fill.

TemplateField.title: TemplateField
TemplateField.description:
Single field of a document template, for dynamic discovery.

Clients should prompt the user for ``USER`` fields, and for ``PROFILE`` fields only
when the referenced party's value is not already available. ``required`` means the
final document needs a non-empty value, regardless of who provides it.

Descriptors are built from a template's annotated input model by
``TemplateFieldBuilder``; composite fields nest their shape in :attr:`fields`
(objects), :attr:`item` (arrays) or :attr:`options` (enums).

TemplateFieldSource.title: TemplateFieldSource
TemplateFieldSource.description:
Where a template field's value comes from.

* ``USER`` — must be entered by the user.
* ``PROFILE`` — taken from a party's profile when available; if the profile is
  incomplete or the party is not registered yet, it must be collected from the user.
* ``COMPUTED`` — generated server-side (current date, reference, derived legal terms).
* ``DEFAULT`` — filled with a boilerplate default; no input required.

TemplateFieldType.title: TemplateFieldType
TemplateFieldType.description:
Structural type of a template field, so clients can render the right input.

Composite types carry their shape: ``OBJECT`` populates :attr:`TemplateField.fields`,
``ARRAY`` populates :attr:`TemplateField.item`, and ``ENUM`` populates
:attr:`TemplateField.options`.

recoveryCodes.description: One-time recovery codes. Store these safely.

UserOperationV06.title: UserOperationV06
UserOperationV06.description:
ERC-4337 v0.6 UserOperation in the JSON-RPC
`eth_sendUserOperation` shape — camelCase keys (via the `CamelModel`
alias generator), `0x`-hex string values for every numeric and byte
field.

All 11 v0.6 fields are present:

    sender, nonce, initCode, callData, callGasLimit,
    verificationGasLimit, preVerificationGas, maxFeePerGas,
    maxPriorityFeePerGas, paymasterAndData, signature

`signature` defaults to `"0x"` because some endpoints
(e.g. `/wallet/op_cosign`) consume an *unsigned* userOp and
produce the signature themselves, while others
(e.g. `/wallet/paymaster_raw`) consume a fully-signed userOp and
submit it on-chain as-is.

UserPreview.title: UserPreview
UserPreview.description: Lightweight user preview (for transaction participants, etc.).
